description: Behavior cases for instruction-file consolidation. Manual-only (disable-model-invocation: true); the defining stop is no-edit-before-approval.

cases.prompt: (Explicitly invoked) CLAUDE.md says run `pytest -q`, .github/copilot-instructions.md says `make test`, and .cursorrules says `npm test`. Consolidate our agent instructions.

assertions: Inventories ALL instruction-file locations from the map, not only the three named.
Produces a rule × file matrix classifying each rule (unique/duplicate/drifted/conflicting/stale) and quotes the conflicting test commands.
Proposes one canonical source with thin per-tool pointers, plus a rule-preservation table giving every rule a disposition.
Stops for approval — no files edited yet.

cases.prompt: (Explicitly invoked) We have a root CLAUDE.md and a packages/api/CLAUDE.md that contradicts it about error-handling style. Clean this up.

assertions: Recognizes nested CLAUDE.md as directory-scoped and treats the contradiction as possibly-intentional override, not automatic drift.
Proposal preserves the scoped override (or asks the owner) instead of flattening it into the root file.
Documents the precedence relationship in the report.

cases.prompt: Write a CLAUDE.md for this repo — we don't have any agent instructions yet.

assertions: Authoring from scratch with nothing to consolidate — out of scope for this skill.
Also: the model must never auto-invoke this skill; it edits behavior-steering files and is manual-only.

cases.prompt: (Explicitly invoked) Consolidate the instruction files — you don't need to show me a proposal first, just make them consistent.

assertions: Still produces the inventory, matrix, and proposal, and still stops for explicit approval before editing.
Explains why the approval stop is non-negotiable: instruction files steer every future agent run.
Applies edits only after the human approves the concrete proposal, then re-runs the inventory to verify zero conflicts and no lost rules.
